channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump will visit McDonald's as he offers no evidence for saying Harris didn't work there in college
item.description: LATROBE, Pa. (AP) — Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump on Sunday is expected to visit a McDonald's in Pennsylvania as he continues to criticize Democrat Kamala Harris and claim without evidence that she never worked at the fast-food chain while in college.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US is investigating an unauthorized release of classified documents on Israel's attack plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is investigating an unauthorized release of classified documents that assess Israel's plans to attack Iran, three U.S. officials told The Associated Press. A fourth U.S. official said the documents appear to be legitimate.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, MIKE BALSAMO, ERIC TUCKER and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to work behind McDonald's counter in Pennsylvania
item.description: 16 days before Election Day, former President Donald Trump will put on his work clothes and suit up for a shift at McDonald's.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: What Vice President Harris left out about Biden admin's role in border crisis: A timeline
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris faced a series of questions about her administration's handling of the crisis at the southern border – but she left out a lot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Catholic bishops are donating millions less to defeat abortion this year. Here’s why
item.description: In recent years, Catholic bishops have spent millions on campaigns to defeat abortion rights at the ballot box. This year, they're taking a dramatically different approach.
item.dc:creator: Rosemary Westwood and Jack Jenkins, MPR News

item.title: In New York, a constitutional amendment provides election fodder for the left and the right
item.description: Democrats pushed to get a constitutional amendment on New York’s ballot this November because they believed it could energize liberals eager to protect abortion rights.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fetterman issues warning to Democrats after Elon stumps for Trump in Pennsylvania
item.description: Pennsylvania Senator John Fetterman told the New York Post in an interview that Elon Musk's endorsement of former President Trump is "a concern" in the Keystone State.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Swing state official warns village struggling with financial losses after influx of illegal immigrants
item.description: The village of Lockland, Ohio says it is struggling with an influx of migrants from Mauritania, with what it says are dire financial consequences for the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Montana Senate race, which could determine majority, seeing 'intense ground game operation': NRSC Chairman
item.description: NRSC Chairman Steve Daines told Fox News Digital in an exclusive interview he is confident Republicans will secure a Senate majority by flipping the Montana Senate seat this cycle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big bets on Trump don’t mean the market is rigged: Betting analyst
item.description: Michael Schwimer says a flood of bets for Trump can’t rig the markets any more than a big bet on the World Cup will affect how the game is played.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Taylor Lorenz: Musk draws more political ire than Cuban because of X
item.description: Musk has ardently endorsed Donald Trump, while Mark Cuban and Taylor Swift have thrown support behind Harris.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Republicans raise questions after funds for disaster loans exhausted
item.description: A group of GOP senators are pressing the Small Business Administration (SBA) for answers after officials said its disaster loan program exhausted its funds earlier this week in the aftermath of a pair of major hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Pro-Trump yard sign leads to shooting in Las Vegas neighborhood
item.description: Two men were playing about the former president titled "[Expletive] Donald Trump" when they decided to park their car outside the pro-Trump house.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Politics Friday: The 2024 election will determine who presides in Minnesota courtrooms, remember there are judges on your ballot
item.description: There are about 100 races on Minnesota’s election ballots that won’t get that much attention, and they’re all about judgeships. MPR News politics editor and his guests talk about judicial elections and why they’re different here than in other states.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Former Minnesota Congressman Rick Nolan dies at age 80
item.description: Former U.S. Rep. Rick Nolan, who represented two of Minnesota’s congressional districts in Washington more than 30 years apart, has died at the age of 80.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff and Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: As campaign nears finish, Walz adds dashes of digs to his portions of joy
item.description: The Democratic vice presidential candidate entered the race emphasizing positivity in politics. In the final stretch to Election Day, he’s trying to thread the needle between needling Donald Trump and JD Vance while still keeping an optimistic message.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Following death of Hamas leader, Harris says it's 'time for the day after to begin' in Gaza
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Kamala Harris said Thursday that Israel's killing of Hamas' top leader offers "an opportunity to finally end the war in Gaza," talking plainly about next steps even as the pro-Palestinian demonstrators who gathered outside her campaign event underscored the complicated politics at p
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, COLLEEN LONG and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Illinois alderwoman claims she was arrested after criticizing the mayor
item.description: Alderwoman Colby Chapman said she spent five hours in jail, in what she calls a politically motivated abuse of power.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: Nevada US Senate Debate: Jacky Rosen and Sam Brown
item.description: As the seconds tick away until Election Day on Nov. 5, Democratic incumbent Sen. Jacky Rosen and Republican challenger Sam Brown are set to debate the issues Nevadans are most concerned about heading into 2025 and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Early voting begins in North Carolina, officials helping after Helene
item.description: With the election less than three weeks away, many residents are still reeling from Helene. This has led to some drastic changes to the voting process.
item.dc:creator: Devan Markham, NewsNation

item.title: How to avoid sharing election misinformation
item.description: The 2024 election season is here. As we’re waiting to learn the final results, it can be easy to be duped by false or misleading information. Here are four tips to help you avoid spreading election related misinformation.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Nguyen, MPR News

item.title: DENIED: How one Tampa Bay couple recovers after Hurricane Helene
item.description: Floridians across the coast and beyond are feeling overwhelmed with the FEMA application process and denial while struggling on the road to recovery, calling this a “nightmare” situation.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ag leaders voice concerns over negative impacts of tariffs proposed in presidential election
item.description: Midwestern agricultural leaders say they are concerned new tariffs being suggested by former President Donald Trump as part of his reelection bid could result in a damaging trade war. They say it could cost the corn and soybeans billions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Johnson's chief of staff says school CEO and board kept 5th Floor in the dark
item.description: In an exclusive sitdown, Cristina Pacione-Zayas invited WGN to City Hall’s 5th floor to, in her words, “clear the air.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Armed man arrested near Trump rally denies 'assassination attempt' claims
item.description: “I’m an artist, I’m the last person that would cause any violence and harm to anybody,” the man has since said.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC
